Double.java

00036 obligated to do so.  If you do not wish to do so, delete this

00038 
00039 package java.lang;
00040 
00052 public final class Double
00053 {
00058     public static final double MAX_VALUE = 1.7976931348623157e+308;
00059 
00064     public static final double MIN_VALUE = 5e-324;
00065 
00070     public static final double NEGATIVE_INFINITY = -1.0 / 0.0;
00071 
00075     public static final double POSITIVE_INFINITY = 1.0 / 0.0;
00076 
00080     public static final double NaN = 0.0 / 0.0;
00081 
00087     private final double value;
00088 
00095     public Double(double value)
00096     {
00097         this.value = value;
00098     }
00099 
00130     public native static String toString(double d);
00131 
00142     public static Double valueOf(String s)
00143         throws NumberFormatException, NullPointerException
00144     {
00145         return new Double(parseDouble(s));
00146     }
00147 
00191     public static double parseDouble(String str)
00192         throws NumberFormatException, NullPointerException
00193     {
00194         String tmp = str.trim(); // Trim all whitespace & control characters
00195         long res, sign = 0;
00196         int i = 0, length = tmp.length();
00197 
00198         if (length == 0) {
00199             throw new NumberFormatException();
00200         }
00201 
00202         char c = tmp.charAt(0);
00203 
00204         // Detect the sign
00205         if (c == '-') {
00206             sign = 0x8000000000000000L;
00207             i++;
00208         } else if (c == '+') {
00209             i++;
00210         }
00211 
00212         tmp = tmp.substring(i);
00213         i = 0;
00214 
00215         if (tmp.equals("NaN")) {
00216             res = 0x7ff8000000000000L;
00217         } else if (tmp.equals("Infinity")) {
00218             res = 0x7ff0000000000000L;
00219         } else {
00220             length = tmp.length();
00221 
00222             if (length == 0) {
00223                 throw new NumberFormatException();
00224             }
00225 
00226             // Parse the integral part (if present)
00227             double value = 0.0;
00228 
00229             while ((i < length) && Character.isDigit(tmp.charAt(i))) {
00230                 value = (value * 10.0)
00231                         + (double) Character.digit(tmp.charAt(i), 10);
00232                 i++;
00233             }
00234 
00235             // Skip the dot (if present)
00236             if ((i < length) && tmp.charAt(i) == '.') {
00237                 i++;
00238             }
00239 
00240             // Parse the fractional part (if present)
00241             double div = 10.0;
00242 
00243             while ((i < length) && Character.isDigit(tmp.charAt(i))) {
00244                 value += ((double) Character.digit(tmp.charAt(i), 10)) / div;
00245                 div *= 10.0;
00246                 i++;
00247             }
00248 
00249             // Parse the exponent
00250             int exponent = 0;
00251             boolean exp_pos = true;
00252 
00253             if ((i < length) && (Character.toLowerCase(tmp.charAt(i)) == 'e')) {
00254                 i++;
00255 
00256                 if (i < length) {
00257                     c = tmp.charAt(i);
00258 
00259                     if (c == '-') {
00260                         i++;
00261                         exp_pos = false;
00262                     } else if (c == '+') {
00263                         i++;
00264                     }
00265                 }
00266 
00267                 int j = i;
00268 
00269                 while ((i < length) && Character.isDigit(tmp.charAt(i))) {
00270                     exponent = (exponent * 10)
00271                                + Character.digit(tmp.charAt(i), 10);
00272                     i++;
00273                 }
00274 
00275                 if (i == j) {
00276                     // After the e/E character there must be an exponent
00277                     throw new NumberFormatException();
00278                 }
00279 
00280                 if (!exp_pos) {
00281                     exponent = -exponent;
00282                 }
00283             }
00284 
00285             // Parse the float/double specifier (if present)
00286             if (i < length) {
00287                 c = Character.toLowerCase(tmp.charAt(i));
00288                 i++;
00289 
00290                 if (c != 'f' && c != 'd') {
00291                     throw new NumberFormatException();
00292                 }
00293             }
00294 
00295             // Ensure that there are no more characters
00296             if (i != length) {
00297                 throw new NumberFormatException();
00298             }
00299 
00300             // Encode the actual number
00301             value *= Math.pow(10.0, exponent);
00302             res = Double.doubleToLongBits(value);
00303         }
00304 
00305         return Double.longBitsToDouble(res | sign);
00306      }
00307 
00315     public static boolean isNaN(double v)
00316     {
00317         // This works since NaN != NaN is the only reflexive inequality
00318         // comparison which returns true.
00319         return v != v;
00320     }
00321 
00330     public static boolean isInfinite(double v)
00331     {
00332         return v == POSITIVE_INFINITY || v == NEGATIVE_INFINITY;
00333     }
00334 
00343     public String toString()
00344     {
00345         return toString(value);
00346     }
00347 
00354     public boolean isNaN()
00355     {
00356         return isNaN(value);
00357     }
00358 
00366     public boolean isInfinite()
00367     {
00368         return isInfinite(value);
00369     }
00370 
00376     public byte byteValue()
00377     {
00378         return (byte) value;
00379     }
00380 
00386     public short shortValue()
00387     {
00388         return (short) value;
00389     }
00390 
00396     public int intValue()
00397     {
00398         return (int) value;
00399     }
00400 
00406     public long longValue()
00407     {
00408         return (long) value;
00409     }
00410 
00416     public float floatValue()
00417     {
00418         return (float) value;
00419     }
00420 
00426     public double doubleValue()
00427     {
00428         return value;
00429     }
00430 
00440     public int hashCode()
00441     {
00442         long v = doubleToLongBits(value);
00443 
00444         return (int) (v ^ (v >>> 32));
00445     }
00446 
00461     public boolean equals(Object obj)
00462     {
00463         if (! (obj instanceof Double))
00464             return false;
00465 
00466         double d = ((Double) obj).value;
00467 
00468         // Avoid call to native method. However, some implementations, like gcj,
00469         // are better off using floatToIntBits(value) == floatToIntBits(f).
00470         // Check common case first, then check NaN and 0.
00471         if (value == d)
00472             return (value != 0) || (1 / value == 1 / d);
00473 
00474         return isNaN(value) && isNaN(d);
00475     }
00476 
00491     public native static long doubleToLongBits(double value);
00492 
00504     public native static double longBitsToDouble(long bits);
00505 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:24 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
